purpose.summary: CBPAUP0C is a batch program that reads pending authorization summary and detail records from an IMS database, checks if the authorization details are expired based on a configurable expiry period, and deletes the expired authorization details. It also deletes authorization summaries if all associated details have been deleted and takes checkpoints periodically.
purpose.business_context: This program is used to purge expired authorization records from the system, likely to reduce database size and improve performance of authorization processing.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Pending authorization summary records from the IMS database.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Pending authorization detail records from the IMS database.

inputs.name: SYSIN
inputs.description: Parameter input containing expiry days, checkpoint frequency, debug flag, and display frequency.

business_rules.description: Authorization details are considered expired if the difference between the current date and the authorization date is greater than or equal to the expiry days specified in the input parameter.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program calculates the difference between the current date (CURRENT-YYDDD) and the authorization date (PA-AUTH-DATE-9C), and compares it with the expiry days (WS-EXPIRY-DAYS).

business_rules.description: If an authorization detail is expired and the authorization response code is '00' (approved), the approved authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks if PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE is '00' and then subtracts 1 from PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and PA-APPROVED-AMT.

business_rules.description: If an authorization detail is expired and the authorization response code is not '00' (declined), the declined authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks if PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE is not '00' and then subtracts 1 from PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT and PA-TRANSACTION-AMT.

business_rules.description: Authorization summary records are deleted if both the approved and declined authorization counts are less than or equal to 0.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks if PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT are both less than or equal to 0.

transforms.transformation_description: Calculates the authorization date by subtracting it from 99999. This is likely done to facilitate date comparisons.
Calculates the difference between the current date and the calculated authorization date.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the pending authorization summary IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the pending authorization detail IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the main control paragraph of the CBPAUP0C program. It orchestrates the process of reading authorization summaries and details, checking for expired authorizations, and deleting them. The paragraph first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to set up the program environment. Then, it enters a main loop that continues until either an error flag (ERR-FLG-ON) is set or the end of the authorization database (END-OF-AUTHDB) is reached. Inside the main loop, it retrieves authorization details using 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL within a nested loop that continues until no more authorization details (NO-MORE-AUTHS) are found for the current summary. For each detail, it calls 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED to determine if the authorization has expired, and if so, calls 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL to delete the detail record. After processing all details for a summary, it checks if both approved and declined authorization counts are less than or equal to zero; if so, it calls 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY to delete the summary record.  Periodically, based on WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT exceeding P-CHKP-FREQ, it calls 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT to take a checkpoint. Finally, after processing all summaries, it calls 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT again and displays summary statistics.

This paragraph initializes the program by accepting the current date and parameters from SYSIN. It accepts the current date and day of the year. It then displays the starting message, the parameters received, and today's date. It checks if the expiry days (P-EXPIRY-DAYS) from the input parameters are numeric; if so, it moves the value to WS-EXPIRY-DAYS; otherwise, it defaults to 5. It also checks if the checkpoint frequency (P-CHKP-FREQ) and display frequency (P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ) are spaces, 0, or low-values; if so, it defaults them to 5 and 10 respectively. Finally, it checks if the debug flag (P-DEBUG-FLAG) is not 'Y'; if so, it sets it to 'N'.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement and serves as the exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It does not perform any specific logic or operations.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization summary record from the IMS database. It first checks if the debug flag is on and displays the current summary read count. It then executes a DLI GN (Get Next) command to read the next PAUTSUM0 segment into PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY. The DIBSTAT value is evaluated to determine the outcome of the read. If DIBSTAT is '  ' (success), it sets NOT-END-OF-AUTHDB to TRUE, increments the summary read count (WS-NO-SUMRY-READ) and the summary process count (WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT), and moves the account ID (PA-ACCT-ID) to WS-CURR-APP-ID. If DIBSTAT is 'GB' (end of database), it sets END-OF-AUTHDB to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, it displays an error message and abends the program by calling 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement and serves as the exit point for the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It does not perform any specific logic or operations.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database for the current summary record. It first checks if the debug flag is on and displays the current detail read count. It then executes a DLI GNP (Get Next within Parent) command to read the next PAUTDTL1 segment into PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. The DIBSTAT value is evaluated to determine the outcome of the read. If DIBSTAT is '  ' (success), it sets MORE-AUTHS to TRUE and increments the detail read count (WS-NO-DTL-READ). If DIBSTAT is 'GE' or 'GB' (end of segment or database), it sets NO-MORE-AUTHS to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, it displays an error message, the summary account ID, the detail read count, and abends the program by calling 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement and serves as the exit point for the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It does not perform any specific logic or operations.

This paragraph checks if the current authorization detail record is expired based on the configured expiry period. It calculates the authorization date (WS-AUTH-DATE) by subtracting the PA-AUTH-DATE-9C from 99999. It then calculates the difference (WS-DAY-DIFF) between the current date (CURRENT-YYDDD) and the calculated authorization date. If the difference is greater than or equal to the expiry days (WS-EXPIRY-DAYS), it sets QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE to TRUE. If the authorization response code (PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE) is '00' (approved), it decrements the approved authorization count (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT) and amount (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT) in the summary record. Otherwise (declined), it decrements the declined authorization count (PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT) and transaction amount (PA-TRANSACTION-AMT) in the summary record. If the authorization is not expired, it sets NOT-QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE to TRUE.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement and serves as the exit point for the 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED paragraph. It does not perform any specific logic or operations.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to '  ', 'GB', or 'GE' in 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY
DIBSTAT not equal to '  ', 'GB', or 'GE' in 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL
